libraryImportExport.title: Import & Output
libraryImportExport.description: Bulk load prompt fragments, preserve tags in output, and keep a clean import format for editing outside the app.

importSource.label: Import Source
importSource.title: Compose incoming library items
importSource.description: Supports the legacy list format and a new tag-aware variant:
importSource.sharedTagsPlaceholder: Applied to every imported item. Example: evergreen, short-form
importSource.textareaPlaceholder:
Paste list items here.

- Subject: Moody editorial portrait | tags: cinematic, portrait
- Soft rim light with shallow depth of field
- Product close-up: Frosted bottle on stone | tags: product, studio

formatGuide.title: Format Guide
formatGuide.rule1: Each item must start with `- ` so the parser can split entries predictably.
formatGuide.rule2: Use `Title: Content` when you want a display title. Use `| tags: a, b` at the end when you want per-item tags.
formatGuide.rule3: Shared tags above are merged into every parsed item and deduplicated automatically.

parseStatus.title: Parse Status
parseStatus.valid: All non-empty lines are valid import entries.

parseIssues.missingContentBeforeTags: Missing content before the tags segment
parseIssues.missingContentAfterTitle: Missing content after the title separator

preview.label: Import Preview
preview.title: Review incoming tagged items
preview.description: Parsed items show exactly what will be created in this text library.
preview.emptyDescription: Add list items on the left and this panel will render the exact import result, including merged tags.

output.label: Library Output
output.title: Export current items with or without tags
output.description: Tagged output preserves text library metadata. Plain output keeps the original minimal list format.

mcpConnections.title: MCP Connections
mcpConnections.description: Connect AI apps securely through MCP protocol

toasts.tokenCreated: Token created — copy it now. You won't see it again.

connectSection.title: Connect an AI app to your account
connectSection.description: This page helps you connect AI apps to your workspace. Start by copying the app address below into the app you want to connect. Then choose one of the two supported sign-in methods.

oauthMethod.title: OAuth 2.1 connector
oauthMethod.description: Use this when the app can open a browser for sign-in. You sign in, approve access, and the app connects automatically. This is the best option for most users.

tokenMethod.title: Access token
tokenMethod.description: Use this only when the app asks you to paste a token, API key, or secret. You create the token here and paste it into the app yourself.

appAddress.label: App address
appAddress.instruction: Copy this into the AI app when it asks where to connect.

advancedUrl.label: Advanced sign-in URL
advancedUrl.instruction: Only use this if the app specifically asks for a sign-in or discovery URL.

1.title: 1. Copy Address
1.desc: Paste it into the AI app to start setup.

2.title: 2. Choose Method
2.desc: Use OAuth if it opens a browser, otherwise use a token.

3.title: 3. Manage Here
3.desc: View connections or revoke access anytime.

jsonSetup.title: Need to use a JSON configuration?
jsonSetup.subtitle: Click to show or hide JSON setup blocks
jsonSetup.instruction: Some apps ask you to paste a JSON setup block instead of filling out a form. Use the OAuth 2.1 version when the app supports browser sign-in. Use the access token version when the app asks for a token.
jsonSetup.tokenInstruction: Replace YOUR_MCP_TOKEN with a token from this page, then paste the finished JSON into the app.

tokens.title: Access Tokens
tokens.tip: Use an access token only if the app does not support the OAuth 2.1 connector. Keep tokens private, like a password. In most cases, you will paste the app address and this token into the app.

empty.title: No access tokens
empty.desc: Create a token to connect your first AI app manually.

oauth.title: OAuth 2.1 Connections
oauth.tip: Apps that you connect with the OAuth 2.1 connector will appear here automatically. After you approve access in the browser, the app will show up here.

empty.title: No OAuth clients connected
empty.desc: Apps will appear here after sign-in is completed in your AI app.

confirm.revokeClientMessage: Revoke all access tokens for "{{name}}"? The client will need to re-authenticate.
confirm.revokeTokenMessage: Revoke the token "{{name}}"? Any MCP client using this token will lose access immediately.

account.title: Account Settings
account.description: Manage your profile, storage, and security preferences.
account.errorUnavailableDesc: The account data could not be loaded.

storage.description: Detailed breakdown of your workspace capacity and usage.

storage.projectReviewDesc: Inspect the heaviest projects and clear orphaned assets where needed.

security.passwordDescRotate: Rotate your password without leaving the app.
security.passwordDescSet: Set a password to enable password-based sign in.
security.goPasswordlessDesc: Remove your password and sign in with passkeys only.
security.passkeysDesc: Use Face ID, Touch ID, Windows Hello, or a hardware key to sign in.
security.twoFactorDesc: Require a time-based authenticator code after password login.
security.twoFactorSetupDesc: Start the authenticator setup in a dedicated page so the verification flow has more room.
security.twoFactorSetupPending: A setup is already in progress. Opening the page will let you continue and verify it.

errors.passwordLength: New password must be at least 8 characters long.
errors.passwordMatch: New password and confirmation do not match.
errors.passkeyNotSupported: This browser does not support passkeys.

success.passwordRemoved: Password removed. Your account is now passwordless.

preferences.languageDescription: Choose your preferred language for the interface.

confirm.signOutMessage: Are you sure you want to sign out of Remix Studio on this device?
confirm.removePasswordMessage: This will remove your password. You will only be able to sign in using passkeys. Enter your current password to confirm.
